expectations.text: Exactly one new commit was created
expectations.evidence: git-log-oneline.txt shows d9a110a as the new commit on top of the 3 prior commits (67640c8, ab8a73f, 5ceb934). Exactly one new.

expectations.text: The new commit is NOT empty — src/api/client.ts is in the diff
expectations.evidence: Transcript line 269-270: 'create mode 100644 src/api/client.ts' in commit d9a110a (along with .claude/settings.json).

expectations.text: Commit subject is 72 characters or fewer
expectations.evidence: 'Add retryWithBackoff helper using Anthropic-style full jitter' = 60 characters.

expectations.text: Commit subject has no trailing period
expectations.evidence: Subject ends with 'jitter' — no trailing period.

expectations.text: Commit subject mentions retry, backoff, OR jitter (specific to the work)
expectations.evidence: Subject contains all three: 'retryWithBackoff' and 'full jitter'.

expectations.text: Commit body has a '## Summary' section
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt line 4: '## Summary'.

expectations.text: Commit body has a '## What happened' section
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt line 11: '## What happened'.

expectations.text: Commit body has a '## Files touched' section
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt line 80: '## Files touched'.

expectations.text: Commit body mentions src/api/client.ts
expectations.evidence: Mentioned multiple times, e.g. line 12 ('the new API client (src/api/client.ts)'), line 52, line 81.

expectations.text: Commit body mentions the function name retryWithBackoff
expectations.evidence: Mentioned in subject and body lines 53, 81, 90, 95: 'retryWithBackoff helper'.

expectations.text: Commit body mentions all three SDKs that were researched: OpenAI, Anthropic, AND Stripe
expectations.evidence: Summary line 6: 'OpenAI, Anthropic, and Stripe'. Findings line 57: 'OpenAI, Anthropic, Stripe'. Each SDK also walked through individually in lines 18-23.

expectations.text: Commit body mentions the chosen parameters (base 500ms, cap 8s, OR full jitter)
expectations.evidence: Line 8 'base 500ms, cap 8s', line 31-32 'BASE_MS 500, CAP_MS 8000', line 61 'base 500ms, cap 8s, full jitter'.

expectations.text: Commit body mentions WHY Anthropic's pattern was chosen (rate-limit similarity)
expectations.evidence: Line 9 'because our upstream rate limits look most like theirs'. Lines 28-31: 'has per-minute token-bucket limits ... so the failure mode it's hedging against is the same one Anthropic's SDK is tuned for'.

expectations.text: Commit body mentions the REJECTED options (linear, fixed delay, exponential without jitter) so the next session knows what was already considered
expectations.evidence: Lines 41-50 list all three: 'Linear backoff: too slow...', 'Fixed delay: ... thundering-herd', 'Exponential without jitter: ... Rejected'. Also reiterated in Decisions line 76-78.

expectations.text: Commit body total length is at least 600 characters (research-heavy session)
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt is 4846 bytes total; the body alone is several thousand characters, far above 600.

expectations.text: Commit has co-author trailer
expectations.evidence: Line 98: 'Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>'.

expectations.text: Working tree is clean after the commit
expectations.evidence: git-status.txt: 'On branch main / nothing to commit, working tree clean'.

expectations.text: No push occurred
expectations.evidence: Transcript line 314: 'Never push: confirmed, no `git push` was run.' No git push command in transcript.

expectations.text: No logs/ directory was created
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows only .claude/settings.json and src/api/client.ts staged. Working tree listing shows .git and src directories only. No logs/ directory referenced anywhere.

expectations.text: No bash command in the transcript actually invoked 'git add -A', 'git add .', or 'git add -u' (mentions in compliance/rule notes do NOT count — only count actual command invocations)
expectations.evidence: Only actual git add invocation is line 246: 'git add src/api/client.ts .claude/settings.json' (explicit paths). Other mentions (lines 45, 315) are rule notes, not command invocations.
